payload.title: Better Bus Service for 125th Street
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)
payload.description: Community workshop about improving crosstown bus service on 125th Street in Manhattan, covering problems, speed metrics, and potential improvements including fare collection, dedicated bus lanes, limited stops, signal priority, and passenger information.

payload.description: Off-board fare collection to speed up boarding
Dedicated bus lanes on 125th Street, including curbside and offset configurations
Limited stop service with fewer stops than local service to reduce travel time

evidence_refs.source_quote: The diagram illustrates two bus routes on 125 th Street... Local... Limited

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

payload.description: Traffic signal priority for buses to reduce time stopped at red lights
Real-time bus tracking information via MTA Bus Time website and smartphone
Limited stop service with fewer stops than local service to reduce travel time

evidence_refs.source_quote: The diagram illustrates two bus routes on 125 th Street, represented by a horizontal line with vertical lines indicating station locations. The top route is labeled 'Local' in a red box and is shown as a solid red line with 10 white circular stops. The bottom route is labeled 'Limited' in a green box and is shown as a solid green line with 4 white circular stops.

issues: evidence_refs[1].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
